message:
fix(pane-interaction): reset all transient state on undo, import, and intelligence mode changes to prevent stale pointer capture and ghost previews

Several pane interaction code paths (undo, redo, snapshot import,
intelligence mode application) were only partially clearing transient
state, leaving stale preview rects, active gestures, live reflow
signatures, and pointer capture adapters behind. This caused:

- Ghost preview rectangles persisting after undo of a structural change
- Pointer capture remaining active after import overwrites the layout
  tree, causing subsequent pointer moves to dispatch against an invalid
  gesture context
- live_reflow_signature not being cleared on early-exit paths in
  apply_drag_semantics when layout solve fails or dock target lookup
  fails, producing incorrect reflow cache hits on subsequent drags
- Intelligence mode being set before confirming operations actually
  applied, leaving the mode indicator out of sync with the tree

Fixes:
- Extract clear_transient_pane_interaction_state() to consistently reset
  preview_state, active_gesture, gesture_timeline_cursor_start, and
  live_reflow_signature in one call
- Extract reset_pointer_capture_adapter() to reinitialize the capture
  adapter with its existing config, clearing any in-flight pointer state
- Extract refresh_next_operation_id_from_timeline() to scan timeline
  entries and set next_operation_id to max+1, ensuring monotonicity
  after import overwrites the timeline
- Apply all three helpers in import, undo, redo, and intelligence mode
  change paths
- Gate intelligence_mode assignment on applied > 0
- Add live_reflow_signature = None to all early-exit paths in
  apply_drag_semantics

New tests:
- runner_core_undo_clears_pointer_capture_after_structural_change
- import_snapshot_resets_capture_and_keeps_operation_ids_monotonic
- apply_drag_semantics_resets_transient_state_when_plan_fails

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
